title: decisions-substrate revision — split into 3 blocks (recognitions / decisions / substrate-conflicts) + uniform field tightening + migrate 49 entries
description: Revise the .project/decisions.json substrate to match the data-room three-artifact frame (per /Users/david/Projects/data-room-pi-context.md and grounding analysis at analysis/2026-05-26-decisions-substrate-revision-grounding.md commit 083630e). Three-block split: (1) recognitions.json — pre-decisions / decision-needs with status enum [recognized | drafted | reviewed | ready_for_enactment | withdrawn]; promotes into decisions.json on user enactment via enacted_from link with recognition retained for lineage; (2) decisions.json — scope-narrowed to ratified entries only, status [enacted | superseded], required enacted_from + enacted_by + enacted_at; block name no longer lies; (3) substrate-conflicts.json — first-class home for in-substrate contradictions (the in-body Amendment/Widening/narrowed prose pattern flagged in the shape survey) with structured per-conflict entries (affected_ids, conflict_kind, resolution_state). Uniform field tightening across all three blocks: writer-identity enum/pattern kind:identifier (kills variance: 'user' / 'human/email' / 'human:email' / bare-email / 'agent/...'); structured commits[]: [{sha, label}] (kills free-text prose-rot commit mentions); null-state convention (empty-array required when field allowed; key-absent forbidden); first-class evolution_log[]: [{date, kind: widening|narrowing|amendment|correction, by, summary, commits[]}] (replaces in-body stacked revisions per feedback_substrate_blocks_not_changelogs). Options_considered preserves existing schema's soft convention (populated only when multiple mandate-compliant candidates genuinely compete); NO rejected_reason requirement added — staleness-prone redundancy with low marginal mandate-004 value (the chosen option's rationale + consequences[] already do the work). Migrate 49 existing entries from .project/decisions.json by current status — current open → recognitions.json with status mapped per drafted-state; current enacted → decisions.json; current superseded → decisions.json status:superseded; in-body revision prose extracted into evolution_log[] for affected entries (DEC-0040 amendment block, DEC-0044 narrowing prose, DEC-0047, DEC-0003 widening). Cascade across consumers: scripts/orchestrator/* (extract-decs.ts, file-block-item.ts, etc.); Pi tools registered in pi-context/index.ts; lens validators in lens-view.ts and roadmap-plan.ts; relations.json edges preserved with target-block updated where ids re-home.
motivation: Address FGAP-113 (decisions-block conflates pre-decisions with decisions; block-name lies; status enum collapses operationally-distinct sub-states). Empirical basis: shape survey at analysis/2026-05-26-decisions-block-shape-survey.md commit 4900a34 — 49 entries, 20 emergent shape-clusters. Canon basis: every revision element traces to established canon (DEC-0040 single source of truth; FGAP-098 cross-reference affordance; FGAP-110 valid/current discipline; FGAP-111 raw-tier-with-decomposition shape; feedback_substrate_blocks_not_changelogs; DispatchContext attestation; data-room three-artifact frame). Nothing here is fresh architectural invention — bringing one of our oldest blocks into compliance with what we've established about substrate-shape since.
acceptance_criteria: Three blocks exist with conformant schemas: recognitions.schema.json + decisions.schema.json (revised) + substrate-conflicts.schema.json; schemas pass AJV meta-validation
Block-api / scripts/orchestrator / Pi tool surfaces exist for each new block per DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface canon (file-block-item, append-relation invocations work; in-pi tools registered)

49 existing decisions.json entries migrated by status: open (e.g. DEC-0001/0002/0003) → recognitions.json with appropriate sub-status; enacted (e.g. DEC-0044/0047) → decisions.json; superseded → decisions.json status:superseded; lineage edges preserved

In-body revision prose extracted into evolution_log[] structured entries for affected entries (DEC-0040, DEC-0044, DEC-0047, DEC-0003) — verifiable via inspection that body fields carry current-truth only and journey lives in evolution_log[]
Writer-identity field uniformly conforms to kind:identifier pattern across all three blocks (zero residual 'user' / 'human/email' / bare-email / 'agent/...' variants)
Commits[] field uniformly structured across all three blocks (zero residual free-text commit mentions in body fields)
Null-state convention enforced (empty-array required where field allowed; AJV catches key-absent)
Relations.json edges referencing migrated entries updated to point to correct destination block; zero dangling references
Lens validators (lens-view.ts + roadmap-plan.ts + status_buckets in context-sdk) handle three-block substrate; zero stale references to single-block model

Cascade complete: scripts/orchestrator/extract-decs.ts handles new shape or is split into per-block extractors; Pi tools updated; CLAUDE.md Project Blocks section updated; SKILL.md regenerated via npm run skills

VER-attested closure: grep verifies zero residual references to pre-revision shape; runtime demo exercises full lifecycle (file recognition → promote to decision → record conflict) end-to-end with real substrate; adversarial probe by fresh-context agent confirms migration completeness
